content: Ari kicked off observability tooling RFP in standup. Two-week eval: DataDog vs New Relic. He'll own the bake-off, engineering will test both against production workloads.
summary: Ari starts DataDog vs New Relic evaluation

content: DataDog pricing: $48k/yr estimated for our scale. New Relic quote pending but likely higher per-seat model. Cost delta will matter for board approval.
summary: DataDog quote $48k/yr vs New Relic TBD

content: Devon and James spent Tuesday morning setting up DataDog sandbox. James had questions about APM config; Devon sorted it. Both noted UI felt cleaner than expected.
summary: Devon + James DataDog sandbox setup, UI praise

content: DataDog strength: pre-built Anthropic + AWS integrations. New Relic requires custom API design work for agent instrumentation. Integration depth matters for observability setup time.
summary: DataDog integrations vs New Relic custom work

content: When evaluating observability tools, load-test both with 72-hour production traffic samples. Measure: dashboard load latency, alert response time, API query cost per million events.
summary: Observability eval: 72h prod test + 3 key metrics

content: Ari sent comparison matrix to Slack. DataDog winning on UX + integrations. New Relic cheaper on licensing but weaker Anthropic integration. Anya flagged cost; I said UX wins outweigh $8k/yr delta.
summary: Ari shares matrix: DataDog UX strong, NR cheaper

content: New Relic's API design for custom instrumentation requires 3–4 week implementation. DataDog SDK auto-detects our Python stack. Time-to-value favors DataDog by ~3 weeks.
summary: DataDog 3-week faster implementation vs New Relic

content: Tom asked about DataDog pricing tiers in standup. I clarified: we're on Pro tier at $48k/yr; Standard would save ~$18k but loses AI features we need for anomaly detection.
summary: Tom asks pricing; Pro tier necessary for AI features

content: DataDog's anomaly detection + forecasting reduces on-call alert noise by ~40% per Ari's test notes. New Relic's alerting is static threshold-based. Operational impact matters beyond cost.
summary: DataDog anomaly detection 40% noise reduction vs NR

content: Anya asked for final cost-benefit writeup. I told her I'd have it Friday; Ari needs to finish performance benchmarks first.
summary: Anya requests cost-benefit writeup, due Friday

content: New Relic licensing: $29k/yr base but requires additional custom ingestion fees (~$8–12k/yr) for our event volume. DataDog's flat $48k all-inclusive. True cost delta is <$5k if NR adds features later.
summary: New Relic true cost $37–41k/yr after custom fees

content: Sebastien asked in 1:1: which tool are we leaning? I said DataDog, UX + integrations beat price on this one. He approved moving to contract review.
summary: Sebastien approves DataDog direction in 1:1

content: For vendor RFCs: always include implementation timeline, integration checklist, and 12-month TCO (not just Year 1 licensing). Prevents sticker-shock surprises during onboarding.
summary: RFC template: timeline, integrations, 12-mo TCO

content: DataDog integrations available: Anthropic, AWS, Cloudflare, Sentry, GitHub. New Relic requires custom wrappers for 3 of 5. Integration debt matters for ops velocity.
summary: DataDog 5/5 native integrations vs New Relic 2/5

content: Legal reviewed DataDog MSA. Three minor red-lines: data retention default (30 days → 90), sub-processor list, and IP indemnity clause. Sent back for standard Clude tweaks.
summary: Legal MSA review: 3 red-lines, sent to DataDog

content: Dashboard design in DataDog is drag-drop, instant. New Relic UI requires JSON config or GUI with nested menus. For observability work, UX compounds over time—team uses it daily.
summary: DataDog UX compounds: drag-drop vs JSON config

content: Ari sent final eval summary: DataDog wins on usability, integration depth, and anomaly detection. New Relic wins on per-seat cost but requires 3 weeks implementation overhead.
summary: Ari final eval: DataDog UX/integrations, NR cheaper

content: I default to choosing tools that reduce context-switching for teams, even if it costs more. DataDog over New Relic felt right because our people spend 2–3 hours/week in observability tooling. UX debt is real debt.
summary: I weight team UX + velocity over pure cost

content: DataDog contract signed Feb 24. Onboarding starts Mar 3. Devon + James own implementation. Estimated full instrumentation: 2 weeks. Go-live target: Mar 17.
summary: DataDog contract signed, Mar 3 onboarding start

content: Post-vendor-selection: document decision rationale in internal wiki with scoring matrix + rejected options + cost-benefit assumptions. Future audits and re-evals need the context.
summary: Post-selection: wiki doc with matrix + assumptions

content: Sent decision summary to Sebastien, Anya, and engineering leads. DataDog wins; full eval details in ops folder. Emphasized 3-week time-to-value vs New Relic's 6+ week custom setup.
summary: Decision summary sent, time-to-value key differentiator

content: Why DataDog over New Relic: (1) $5k TCO delta negligible, (2) 3-week faster implementation, (3) native Anthropic + AWS integrations, (4) team feedback on UX consistency. Decision defensible for 18+ months.
summary: DataDog decision: cost-neutral, 3-week faster, integrations
